title: Understanding Angular Momentum Conservation
text: The principle of conservation of angular momentum states that if no external torque acts on a system, the total angular momentum of the system remains constant. This means that the initial angular momentum before the tuck is equal to the angular momentum during the somersaults.

title: Angular Momentum Formula
text: Angular momentum (L) is given by the product of the moment of inertia (I) and the angular velocity (ω): $$L=I \omega$$. Use this formula to find the initial angular velocity.

title: Calculate Initial Angular Momentum
text: Calculate the initial angular momentum using the moment of inertia before the tuck and the unknown initial angular velocity.

title: Calculate Angular Momentum During Somersaults
text: Calculate the angular momentum during the somersaults using the moment of inertia after the tuck and the angular velocity during the somersaults.

title: Relate Initial and Somersault Angular Momentum
text: Since angular momentum is conserved, set the initial angular momentum equal to the angular momentum during the somersaults.

title: Find Angular Velocity During Somersaults
text: Use the time taken for the somersaults to find the angular velocity during the somersaults. The diver completes three somersaults in $$1.4$$ seconds.

title: Solve for Initial Angular Velocity
text: Substitute the expression for $$\omega_{somersaults}$$ into the conservation equation and solve for $$\omega_{initial}$$.

title: Calculate the Initial Rotation Rate
text: Calculate the initial rotation rate using the expression derived.
